Respiratory tract, bronchial brushing or EBUS-FNA specimen
PSC Category VI: Malignant — small cell carcinoma

Loose clusters and single small blue cells show nuclear molding, crush artifact, scant cytoplasm, very high N:C ratio, finely granular salt-and-pepper chromatin, absent nucleoli, and apoptotic bodies.
Necrosis, apoptotic debris and crush artifact; synaptophysin, chromogranin, CD56, TTF-1 positive; Ki-67 very high.
Nuclear molding with salt-and-pepper chromatin distinguishes this vs Lymphocyte, which lacks crush artifact and neuroendocrine marker expression.
Serous cavity, pleural/peritoneal/pericardial effusion cytology specimen
TIS Category V: MAL-S — metastatic adenocarcinoma NOS
Tight three-dimensional clusters, acini, papillae, or signet-ring cells show enlarged irregular nuclei, coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli, high N:C ratio, and mucin-vacuolated cytoplasm.
Mucinous or necrotic effusion; claudin-4, MOC31, BerEP4, CEA positive; calretinin/WT1/D2-40 negative.
Claudin-4-positive glandular clusters with mucin distinguish this vs Mesothelioma, which is claudin-4 negative and shows mesothelial windows.
Thyroid gland, ultrasound-guided fine-needle aspiration specimen
Bethesda V: Suspicious for malignancy — indeterminate malignant features

Limited clusters or papillary/microfollicular fragments show focal malignant-type nuclear enlargement, irregular membranes, grooves or nucleoli, increased N:C ratio, and scant-to-moderate delicate cytoplasm.
Scant colloid; CK19/HBME-1/galectin-3, calcitonin, or lymphoid markers selected by suspected tumor type.
Strong but insufficient malignant features distinguish this vs Bethesda VI Malignant, which has unequivocal cytology and confirmatory lineage.
Thyroid gland, ultrasound-guided FNA or cell-block specimen
Bethesda VI: Malignant — thyroid malignant tumor

Cellular cohesive or discohesive malignant population shows marked nuclear enlargement, irregular membranes, coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli or grooves, high N:C ratio, mitoses, and lineage-dependent cytoplasm.
Necrosis or tumor matrix possible; PAX8/TTF-1/thyroglobulin, calcitonin, cytokeratin, or lymphoid markers classify lineage.
Unequivocal malignant cytology with lineage marker support distinguishes this vs Bethesda V Suspicious, which is limited or qualitatively incomplete.
